ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Singaporean prawn nugget on a stick; closest parent is shrimp nugget food product (FOODON_03315091).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for prawn nugget skewer; A0CKM (processed crustacean product) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific fried prawn snack product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is a single prawn nugget on a stick weighing approximately 45 g, as sold by Old Chang Kee at SGD 2.40 per piece.

nutrient_source.title: Fried prawn / shrimp cake (har cheong) — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient reference; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central fried breaded shrimp (FDC ID 175180) for micronutrient estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo does not list Old Chang Kee Prawn Nugget OnStik as a named entry. Values are adapted from HPB Foodo fried prawn cake / har cheong category (closest Singaporean analogue) and cross-checked against USDA FDC fried breaded shrimp (FDC 175180). Serving size estimated at 45 g based on typical Old Chang Kee single-piece snack weight. Per-100 g values derived accordingly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried battered seafood / fish cake products — analogous estimate based on battered fried fish cake GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Old Chang Kee Prawn Nugget OnStik. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for battered fried fish cake / seafood fritter products, which share a similar batter-to-protein ratio and frying method. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 9.45) / 100 ≈ 5.2, where carbohydrate per 45 g serving = 21.0 × 0.45 = 9.45 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (520 mg per 100 g; approximately 234 mg per piece)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of prawn nuggets on a stick due to the high sodium content (~234 mg per piece), which contributes meaningfully toward the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg sodium; consuming multiple pieces in one sitting may significantly elevate daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) and refined starch batter coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume prawn nuggets on a stick in moderation; the low GI (~55) and refined wheat-starch batter contribute approximately 9.5 g of carbohydrate per piece, and the deep-frying process adds fat that may slow glucose absorption but also increases caloric density.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation with saturated fat and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit intake of deep-fried prawn nuggets, as each piece provides approximately 5.9 g of total fat including 1.3 g saturated fat and 234 mg sodium, contributing to both atherogenic lipid load and blood pressure elevation with regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prawn Nugget On A Stick (also marketed as 'Prawn Nugget OnStik') is a popular Singaporean deep-fried snack sold by Old Chang Kee, a well-known local snack chain. It consists of a seasoned prawn and fish paste mixture coated in a light wheat-starch batter, shaped into a nugget form, skewered on a stick, and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The exterior is crunchy and lightly oily, while the interior is dense, springy, and savoury with a pronounced prawn flavour. It is typically sold as a single-piece grab-and-go snack priced at approximately SGD 2.40. Nutritionally, each piece (approximately 45 g) provides around 112 kcal, 5.6 g protein, 5.9 g fat, and 9.5 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at approximately 234 mg per piece, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The low glycemic index (~55) means it has a relatively low impact on blood glucose, relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. It contains common allergens including crustaceans, gluten, egg, and fish. The snack is considered halal and dairy-free. It is best consumed fresh and hot, and is a staple of Singaporean hawker and mall food culture.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo entry exists for Old Chang Kee Prawn Nugget OnStik by name. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest HPB Foodo Singaporean fried prawn cake analogue and cross-checked with USDA FDC fried breaded shrimp data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for battered fried seafood products. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the absence of a direct named match in any primary approved source.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo fried prawn cake reference or the USDA FDC fried breaded shrimp entry used for this adaptation. All other -1 values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated numeric values from the adapted sources. Serving size of 45 g is an estimate based on typical Old Chang Kee single-piece snack weight; the actual weight may vary slightly by outlet. The food is classified under the Singaporean cuisine category and therefore requires English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi multilingual coverage, all of which have been provided. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate, categorized as low; a direct GI measurement for this specific product has not been published in peer-reviewed literature as of the processing date.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record from HPB Foodo adapted source and USDA FDC cross-check. All mandatory sections populated.

Updated gi_source.source_match_notes, health_context.type2_diabetes key_factor and guidance, llm_training.natural_language_description, and notes to reflect the 'low' GI category. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed all Tamil (இறால் நகெட் குச்சி) and Hindi (झींगा नगेट स्टिक) entries appear verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed vitamin_d_mcg -1 documented in notes. Confirmed GI numeric and >0. Confirmed ontology match_type values are only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none'. All hard gates and Q1–Q8 verified as passing.
